qa_pairs.question: Why is it recommended to specify flavours and images using their IDs rather than their names when configuring resources with Terraform?
qa_pairs.answer: It is recommended to use IDs because they uniquely define the state of the resource and will never change. In contrast, if a flavour or image name changes (e.g., due to retirement and replacement with another of the same name), Terraform might detect the updated ID and determine that the associated resource needs to be rebuilt or resized, which is a destructive operation.

qa_pairs.question: What destructive operation can occur if a Terraform configuration uses the name of an OpenStack flavour or image, and that name is later updated or reassigned?
qa_pairs.answer: If a flavour or image name used in a Terraform configuration is updated or reassigned to a different underlying resource, Terraform will detect the change in the resource's ID. This can lead Terraform to determine that it needs to rebuild or resize the associated resource, which is a destructive (and reconstructive) operation.
